resources.string: Permission needed — tap the button above.
Review available — freeze still active. Confirm to change.
Reboot detected — freeze stays LOCKED until real time is verified from the network.\nConnect to the internet to verify. (Extending is still allowed.)
Enter a positive number of minutes.
Built-in test domain — cannot be removed.
This page is blocked during your freeze.
Reads the address bar of browsers to block pages on your blocklist and enforce safe-search, but only while a freeze is running. No browsing data is stored or sent anywhere.

This feature uses Android's Accessibility service to read the URL shown in your browser's address bar.

What it does with that text:
• Compares the current URL against your local blocklist (shared with the VPN feature).
• If it matches, it navigates the browser away from the page and shows a brief "Blocked" notice.
• For Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo searches it enforces safe-search, and blocks searches containing banned keywords.

Important guarantees:
• It only reads the address bar of browser apps, and only while a freeze is running.
• It does NOT log, store, or transmit your browsing history — matching happens entirely on-device.
• This is a separate, optional module. You can turn it off any time in Accessibility settings without affecting the rest of the app.
Status: not enabled — tap below to turn it on.
Update failed — keeping the current list.
